methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU-AI Ready RVA partnership signals intentional concentration of AI capability in Richmond metro hub, creating structured educational pathways that could deepen expertise but currently lacks rural distribution mechanism to prevent capability clustering.

signals.title: ODU and Google launch AI incubator to boost innovation in Hampton Roads - 13newsnow.com
signals.signal_reading: ODU-Google AI incubator establishes Hampton Roads as a third capability pole alongside NoVA and Richmond, reinforcing the three-metro concentration pattern while leaving Valley, Southside, and Southwest regions without comparable deep AI infrastructure.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents rare distributed capability-building outside metro hubs, signaling potential for peer network learning models that could spread deep skills into historically underserved Appalachian communities.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA schools grappling with AI classroom integration reflects shallow, platform-mediated adoption where students consume AI tools without necessarily building deep technical capability—distributed access but uncertain depth of understanding.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: MLS introducing AI-powered home search exemplifies shallow distribution pattern where consumer-facing platforms deliver AI functionality broadly across Hampton Roads without requiring users to develop underlying AI literacy or technical skills.
